displayPreferences.toolbarAria: Currency (CLP or USD) and MTD/YTD period

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.body: The app hit an unexpected error while rendering this view.

auth.title: Sign in
auth.subtitle: Demo with synthetic data. Sign in with any email and the shared password.

errors.unknown: Could not sign in. Try again.

mortgageUf.title: Mortgage cuota — UF timing
mortgageUf.waitMessage: Don't pay the dividendo yet — the UF is {{trend}}. Wait past the cierre on {{cierre}} so the charge lands on the {{nextMonth}} facturación. Best known day: {{bestDate}} (UF {{ufBest}} vs {{ufNow}} today).
mortgageUf.horizonNote: The UF after {{horizon}} isn't published yet (comes ~the 9th) — a later day could be cheaper still.
mortgageUf.payTodayMessage: Pay the dividendo today — from here the UF turns up (or the window ends). The charge lands on the {{nextMonth}} facturación.

fxCoverage.title: USD/CLP exchange rate incomplete
fxCoverage.missingFromDate: No exchange rate since {{date}} to convert balances and charts to US$.
fxCoverage.sparseHistory: Sparse daily history ({{daily}} days observed out of {{rows}} rows in fx_daily).
fxCoverage.hint: Run the backfill or check the sync at
fxCoverage.depositConversionError: At least one deposit could not be converted to US$ (missing fx_daily on the movement date).
fxCoverage.syncStale: The USD/EUR sync with Banco Central is stale or failed.
fxCoverage.yahooRejected: {{count}} Yahoo CLP=X date(s) rejected (invalid exchange rate): {{dates}}. Conversions use the last prior valid rate.
fxCoverage.warningBuyRateMissing: CLP→US$ conversion without a buy rate in fx_daily_bid_ask; the reference mid rate was used.
fxCoverage.warningSellRateMissing: US$→CLP conversion without a sell rate in fx_daily_bid_ask; the reference mid rate was used.
fxCoverage.warningUsdReferenceClp: The CLP amount is a reference (mid rate); the US$ capital is the canonical value.
fxCoverage.cardMissingFx: No exchange rate for this date — cannot display the US$ value

rates.instrumentDisplayHint: In CLP the USD/CLP series converts dollar closes; fund unit values start in CLP and are then converted to US$ in USD mode.

fx.gapsHint: Rows inferred from the Yahoo mid rate (±2 CLP) or with no data on that date. Enter your broker's real rate and save.
fx.gapsEmpty: No dates pending review for US$ buy/sell.
fx.gapsInvalidValues: Buy and sell must be positive numbers.
fx.gapsBuySellOrder: Buy must be ≥ sell (more CLP per US$ when buying).

realEstate.mortgageImportNotice: UF mortgage: export the <1>dividendos</1> sheet from Numbers to <3>cfraser/depto-dividendos.csv</3>. The import creates <5>one movement per real payment</5> (same date as at the bank), with CLP, payment UF, UF/day, remaining credit, amortization, interest, the <7>min UF</7> scenario and <9>net value</9> / <11>accumulated payment</11> totals in the note. The real-estate account page shows the table aligned to that sheet. In charts, <13>accum. contributions in CLP</13> is the sum of the pesos paid; in <15>USD</15> (if you use that view) each payment's equivalent at the payment-day rate (5 decimals) is added, without reconverting the CLP total at each month's rate. Same for <17>UF</17> in APIs that request the UF unit. Home value and liability still come from the Excel at month end.

charts.noMonthlyPl: No monthly P/L data for this period.
charts.noValuationSeries: No valuation series for this period.
charts.noRecentValuations: No recent valuations to build the chart.
charts.groupPerfHint: Bars per account or subgroup, YTD area (sum of monthly Δ over the calendar year), diamond = total monthly Δ. Derived.
charts.groupPerfComboTitle: Δ by account / subgroup, combined YTD and total Δ
charts.groupAccumHint: One bar = the class's monthly sum. Continuous area (no per-year bands). From the first month with data.

sections.netWorthUsdSectionTitle: Net worth, invested and US$ levels (CLP)
sections.netWorthUsdSectionHint: Net worth and invested in CLP. Dashed lines: CLP equivalent of US$10k, US$50k, US$100k, US$250k and US$500k at each date's exchange rate (US$ × that day's USD/CLP). The Y axis tracks only net worth and invested; the US$ levels may fall out of scale.
sections.perfSectionHintMonthly: Bars per class (retirement / brokerage), investments YTD area and diamond = combined monthly Δ.
sections.perfSectionHintYearly: Bars per class (retirement / brokerage), annual investments total and diamond = combined annual Δ.
sections.perfChartTitleMonthly: Monthly Δ by class, investments YTD and combined monthly Δ
sections.perfChartTitleYearly: Annual Δ by class, annual investments total and combined annual Δ
sections.accumSectionHintMonthly: Top: accumulated earnings with monthly Δ and MA3. Bottom: monthly Δ, deposits and their MA3 (same scale).
sections.accumSectionHintYearly: Top: accumulated earnings with annual Δ and MA3. Bottom: annual Δ, deposits and their MA3 (same scale).

allocation.title: Allocation (latest valuations)
allocation.empty: Add accounts and valuations to see the chart.

dashboard.accountsTreeEmpty: No accounts yet. Open an asset tab and note the category IDs for POST /api/accounts.
dashboard.monthlyDetailHint: Totals per month (sum of net-worth accounts).
dashboard.flowsHint: Movements across all net-worth accounts, ordered by date.

cardBreakdown.summaryAria: Deposits and total and period Δ

settings.pageHint: Display preferences saved in this browser.

watchlist.pageHint: App instruments and manually added symbols. Choose which ones to show in the top strip.

panelAccounts.pageHint: Reference view: accounts and the same Net worth hierarchy as the sidebar (portfolio_groups).
panelAccounts.excludeHint: Checked accounts are left out of bucket totals and Liabilities. Credit cards also stop netting against Savings and reserves.
panelAccounts.deleteConfirm: Delete account #{{id}} ({{name}})? This cannot be undone.
panelAccounts.emptyTree: The net-worth master bucket was not found in the navigation tree.

addAccount.initialMovementsHint: Same logic as SPY/VEA: flow_kind, CLP/USD amounts and shares (units_delta) where applicable.
addAccount.noMovements: No initial movements. The account would stay empty until you import or load movements.
addAccount.createSuccess: Account created (#{{id}}). The navigation tree was refreshed; run the NYSE sync if you need unit-value history.
addAccount.previewInvalid: Fill in name, ticker, category, and a date on every movement row.

notifications.pageHint: Validation notices (e.g. Fintual) and other system alerts.

importSync.pageHint: Automatic sync status, run log, and coverage of imported monthly documents.
importSync.availableDocumentsHint: One row per month (from the current month backwards). Columns: accounts with a monthly statement. Cards with USD show usd and clp subcolumns under the card name. ✓ = data imported; links to the PDF when the file is on disk. ✓✗ = imported but no PDF linked. ✗ = the month's document is missing.
importSync.availableDocumentsEmpty: No accounts with monthly documents configured.

genericUniqueMerchants.hint: Merchants tagged as One-off on import (exact name match, no prefix rules). Adding or editing applies the backfill to existing movements.
genericUniqueMerchants.addPlaceholder: Merchant name (as it appears on the statement)
genericUniqueMerchants.confirmDelete: Remove “{{merchant}}” from the list? Expenses already tagged as One-off are not modified.

importSync.cartolaSinMovimientosOnFile: {{account}}: statement on disk with no movements ({{month}})

accountDetail.positionHint: Stocks and crypto: ticker and units (shares or coin) from movements in the database (<1>movements.units_delta</1> and import notes).
accountDetail.positionHintAfp: AFP UNO Fondo A: units from <1>movements.units_delta</1> (certificate in <2>cfraser/afp-uno-certificado-cotizaciones.csv</2> or <3>.txt</3> when running <4>import:excel</4>, or <5>npm run afp:uno:cert-sync</5>).
accountDetail.sameBaseAsValuation: Same base as valuation and contributions.

brokerageMovements.title: Add movements
brokerageMovements.hint: Same logic as SPY/VEA: flow_kind, CLP/USD amounts and shares (units_delta) where applicable. Optional: destination/source account for single-row transfers.
brokerageMovements.emptyDraft: No drafts. Use the buttons to add rows, then save.

usdCashMovements.title: Add movements
usdCashMovements.hint: Pick the type on each row: CLP deposit, USD purchase / CLP sale (both amounts), dividend (from a stock), interest / returns (in USD), withdrawals, or other.
usdCashMovements.emptyDraft: No drafts. Add deposits, USD purchases, or transfers to another account.

clpCashMovements.title: Add movements
clpCashMovements.hint: Pick the type on each row: CLP deposit, interest / returns (in CLP), withdrawal, or other. Interest raises the balance but does not count as your own contribution (it is a return). Optional: counterpart account on deposits/withdrawals to record the transfer between accounts in a single row.
clpCashMovements.emptyDraft: No drafts. Add deposits, interest, or withdrawals.

unitsFlow.title: Add contribution / withdrawal
unitsFlow.hint: Contribution or withdrawal with a counterpart. Enter the CLP amount and the {{unit}}; the amount must match {{unit}} × unit value (otherwise it is rejected).
unitsFlow.emptyDraft: No drafts. Add a row and fill in date, amount, units, and counterpart.
unitsFlow.invalid: Each row needs a date (YYYY-MM-DD), a CLP amount, units, and a counterpart account.

bookLedger.sectionHint: Valuation = balance on a date (valuations table). Flow = a CLP contribution or withdrawal (movements table). Positive amount = inflow; negative = withdrawal.
bookLedger.valuationHint: One balance point per date. Use 0 to close the account after a full withdrawal.
bookLedger.movementHint: Signed CLP contributions and withdrawals (+ inflow, − withdrawal).
bookLedger.movementEmptyDraft: No drafts. Add a row, fill in date and amount, then save.
bookLedger.movementInvalid: Check the date (YYYY-MM-DD) and a nonzero amount on every row.

mortgagePayment.sectionHint: Enter the amounts from the bank's statement. Amortization, the UF balance, and the property's net value are computed in the app (daily UF from uf_daily).
mortgagePayment.minUfHint: From the statement — used to split scheduled amortization from any prepayment.
mortgagePayment.desgravamenOverride: Use the bank's value (instead of the computed one)
mortgagePayment.amortExtOverride: Enter the prepayment directly (instead of splitting from the minimum)
mortgagePayment.invalid: Fill in the date and valid CLP amounts.

mortgageSheet.intro: Table read from the mortgage book in SQLite (<1>depto_dividendos_sheet_rows</1>): every row with a CLP amount is a payment (a month can have several).
mortgageSheet.pieNote: The <1>down payment</1> is on the property account (suecia), not on the mortgage liability.
mortgageSheet.tasaNote: The loan's annual rate in the model is <1>4.95%</1>.
mortgageSheet.scenarioIntro: Scenarios from the apartment sheet (not movements), on mortgage-calendar dates (the 11th of each month). The first row is the next projected installment. The minimum payment (30 years) is editable in Numbers; the maximum (~80 UF) approximates paying off in 5 years at the start of the loan.

import.pastePlaceholder: Paste movements copied from the bank's website (date, merchant, amount per line or tab-separated)
import.ccWebPasteHint: Paste the recent-movements list before the PDF statement is available. Duplicates are skipped.
import.ccStatementPdfHint: Upload the CLP PDF and, if applicable, the international US$ PDF. It merges with what was already imported.
import.cuentaVistaWebPasteHint: One movement per line: “date; description; amount” (negative amount = charge) or “date; description; charge; credit”. Date with year (dd/mm/yyyy). E.g. 15/06/2026; Traspaso Internet a Cta. Cte.; -5.000. Duplicates and rows already covered by a bank statement or an internal transfer are skipped.
import.checkingRecentHint: Checking-account “latest movements” export. Files with already-imported rows can be re-uploaded.
import.checkingCartolaHint: Full monthly bank statement. If the month was already imported, only new movements are added.

checking.cartolaMonthHint: One row per month of history. Deposits and charges only with an imported statement. Month-end balance = sum of movements (ledger). Statement balance = the closing printed on the statement (reference only). Difference = month-end balance − statement balance.
checking.cartolaMonthEmpty: No months in movements or valuations. Run npm run import:checking-cartolas on the server.
checking.cartolaMonthImportedCount: {{imported}} of {{total}} month(s) with an imported statement
checking.cartolaRegisteredNoMovements: Statement registered with no imported movements
checking.ledgerAnchorHint: One-time adjustment at the start of the history (the month before the first) to align the ledger with the final balance of the last imported statement. Recomputed when importing statements or adding manual movements.
checking.ledgerAnchorDerived: Statement target {{month}}: {{amount}} · suggested date {{date}} (month before the first)
checking.ledgerAnchorNoCartola: No statement with a final balance — no automatic anchor.

creditCard.historialHint: Lines: installment debt and total balance (same criteria as Monthly detail). Bars: that billing month's payment due (due the following month) and the amount billed at close. Future months: nothing billed (nothing invoiced yet) and projected balance = installments still owed at that month-end — a cycle's installments leave the debt on their pay-by (~10th of the following month), not at the close that bills them.
creditCard.financingSectionHint: Same data as the Billing cycles table. Stacked bars = CLP billed + US$ billed (in CLP). Financing cost = statement interest and fees + implicit interest on installment plans with rate > 0% (0% plans contribute nothing).
creditCard.financingChartTitle: Billed and financing cost by month
creditCard.financingChartEmpty: No billing months or financing cost to chart.
creditCard.financingChartTooltipHint: Billed = statement close; financing cost = what the bank charges (excludes interest-free installment purchases).
creditCard.flowsHint: Contributions, withdrawals, and movements recorded on the account.
creditCard.configHint: Credit limit and billing cycle for this card. An empty cycle end defaults to day 20.
creditCard.configInvalidCupo: Invalid limit: use a number ≥ 0 (CLP without decimals) or leave the field empty.
creditCard.configInvalidCycleDay: Invalid cycle day: integer between 1 and 31.
creditCard.detallePorMesBillingHint: One row per billing month. Total billed = statement close (~20th); in the open month (no PDF yet) it includes the carry-over from the previous closed month plus the month's charges, minus payments (PAGO) until the next close. Total balance = billed + installment debt − next month's payment due.
creditCard.colTotalFacturadoProjectedHint: Projected value: estimated payment due for this billing month (no statement close yet).
creditCard.facturacionesHint: One row per billing month (close ~20th). Payment due = charges due in the “pay by” month (~10th of the following month).
creditCard.proxyEarningsHint: Estimated gain from investing the amount at each due date. Projected with CPI when there is no real price.
creditCard.facturacionModalEmpty: No movements imported for this month.
creditCard.facturacionDeleteConfirmBody: Delete “{{merchant}}” ({{amount}}) from this billing month? This cannot be undone.
creditCard.associatedCardsHint: Physical card numbers with statements imported into this account.

accountDetail.monthlyPerfIntro: Two charts: (1) monthly P/L vs <1>YTD</1> (the area restarts every January). (2) the same monthly Δ with an <3>Accumulated earnings</3> area (continuous from the first month, no per-year bands). The table keeps the detail.
accountDetail.monthlyPerfMortgageNote: For the mortgage, <1>month P/L</1> = net contributions − CLP balance drop (UF cost + interest vs visible amortization), not the investment formula.
accountDetail.monthlyPerfCcLedgerNote: This account's monthly valuations are written to the database when importing the PDF's CSV; class tabs (e.g. Liabilities) read the same table.
accountDetail.monthlyPerfNotEnough: Not enough months of monthly valuation to compute changes (or the account only has one point).

stateContribution.title: APV-A state contribution
stateContribution.intro: State bonus (~15% of your previous year's deposits, capped). Accumulated total: <1>{{total}}</1>

periodReturns.title: Returns
periodReturns.hint: Flow-adjusted chained returns: contributions and withdrawals do not count as gains. Each period compounds the monthly returns; the amount is the period's nominal gain or loss in the active unit.
periodReturns.insufficientHistory: Not enough history for this period

groupPage.notFound: No group view found for this route.
groupPage.monthlyDetailHint: Group totals per month (sum of the child accounts).
groupPage.monthlyDetailHintLiabilities: Group totals per month (sum of every account under this node, including cards and the mortgage).
groupPage.monthlyDetailEmpty: No monthly performance data for this group's accounts.
groupPage.flowsHint: Movements across all child accounts, ordered by date.
groupPage.flowsHintLiabilities: Movements across all accounts under this node (cards, mortgage, etc.), ordered by date.
groupPage.accountsTreeTitleLiabilities: Groups and accounts in this view
groupPage.chartToggleAccumulatedDepositsTitle: Dashed lines with accumulated contributions per account or group (same scale as valuation).
groupPage.accountsTreeEmpty: No accounts in this view yet.

pasivos.creditCardSectionHint: Aggregated totals for every card under this node (billing cycles, installment debt, and financing cost).
pasivos.mortgageSectionHint: Installments, balance, and financing cost from the payments spreadsheet.
pasivos.mortgagePerfHint: For the mortgage, month P/L = financing cost (interest and UF variation), not the investment formula.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: Income versus expenses and net deposits. Mortgage payments are split with the existing breakdown: interest + insurance counts as expense and amortization as deposit (no double counting). AFP+AFC contributions (payroll deductions, they never pass through the checking account) are shown separately and do not subtract from the net; withdrawals do count because they return to the post-tax circuit.

income.intro: Income from bank-statement credits in checking and cuenta vista accounts, excluding internal transfers and deposits to investments.
income.empty: No bank-statement income or manual entries.
income.monthlyDetailHint: One row per calendar month. Salary and severance come from payslips linked to bank-statement credits; unclassified includes the rest.
income.excludeConfirmMessage: Exclude the {{date}} credit ({{description}}) from Income? It will not count in totals or charts.
income.sectionExcludedHint: Bank-statement credits marked as non-income. Restoring them puts them back in totals and charts.
income.sectionFilteredHint: Bank-statement credits skipped by automatic rules (transfers, investment returns, etc.). Including one forces it into Income.
income.includeConfirmMessage: Include the {{date}} credit ({{description}}) in Income even though it matches an automatic filter?

workEarnings.sectionHint: Breakdown imported from payslip PDFs. The net pay must match a linked bank-statement credit.
workEarnings.empty: No payslips imported. Run the payroll parse and import.

deposits.intro: Net external capital by category (positive deposits, negative withdrawals). Same timeline as the “contributions” in accounts and charts: movements plus CLP wires and brokerage withdrawals.

depositsReconciliation.title: Deposits reconciliation
depositsReconciliation.intro: Positive movements in net-worth accounts classified by coverage in the expense ledger.
depositsReconciliation.sectionResolvedInternalTransfer: Internal transfer between accounts (not through checking)
depositsReconciliation.emptyResolvedFamilyFunded: No deposits marked as family contributions.
depositsReconciliation.emptyResolvedInternalTransfer: No internal transfers between net-worth accounts.
depositsReconciliation.emptyNoCheckingSource: No deposits in months without a bank statement.
depositsReconciliation.emptyNeedsAttention: Every deposit is linked or has no bank statement.
depositsReconciliation.assertedTitle: Manually marked as Deposits — no match
depositsReconciliation.assertedIntro: Checking outflows you categorized as Deposits (asserting that an equivalent deposit exists) but without a unique candidate: with 2+ candidates it is ambiguous and nothing is guessed; with 0 the deposit is missing from the ledger.
depositsReconciliation.assertedEmpty: No outflows marked as Deposits without a match.
depositsReconciliation.assertedLinkedSummary: {{count}} outflow(s) manually marked as Deposits were already linked automatically.
depositsReconciliation.redemptionsIntro: Capital that leaves a net-worth account (not checking) and returns to checking: fund redemptions, DAP maturities, withdrawals. They are linked to the checking credit that is already excluded from income.
depositsReconciliation.redemptionEmptyNoCheckingSource: No redemptions in months without a bank statement.
depositsReconciliation.redemptionEmptyNeedsAttention: Every redemption is linked or has no bank statement.

expenses.realEstateIntro: Per-place bills linked to the real purchases that paid them (card or checking). A bill's charge can land the same month or one or two months later; amounts use the linked purchase or, if missing, the expected value. Use “Assign purchase…” to link history purchase-first.

realEstate.noCandidates: No purchases with the same amount in the expected payment month (same month or up to 2 months after the bill).
realEstate.deleteEntryConfirm: Delete this bill row? Its link is removed too.

creditCard.intro: Expenses by the purchase's calendar month (credit card) plus checking withdrawals that count as expenses, in CLP. Opening a month shows Purchases (one-off charges and installment-purchase totals) and Installments (installments billed that month). In “Per installment” mode each installment counts in its own month; in “Total” the full amount counts in the purchase month. Internal transfers and card payments are excluded.

financing.title: Installment financing of billed amounts
financing.intro: Link a billed amount (e.g. Líder) that you paid in installments with the Santander card. In “Total” the billed amount shows in its month; in “Per installment” each expense is spread across the payment's installments.

creditCard.monthlyDetailHint: One row per calendar month. Purchases: the purchase month (one-off charges and installment totals). Installments: the month each installment is billed. Month spend excludes “Doesn't count” and installment 0.
creditCard.monthModalEmpty: No movements in this calendar month.
creditCard.modalSectionExcludedHint: Installment 0, installment-purchase totals (Per-installment mode), the Doesn't count / Deposits categories, and credit notes. They do not add to the month's spend.
creditCard.unclassifiedTableHint: Uncategorized charges, ordered by amount (largest first). Assign a category here or per merchant in the month detail.
creditCard.depositMatchedTableHint: Charges matched to an investment deposit: checking (auto:deposit-match note) or a card linked to a Suecia installment (amortization in the chart). Review possible false positives among transfers.

bigGroups.bulkSelectAria: Big group for the selected purchases
bigGroups.chartFilterHint: Chart without big groups marked as excluded; the table shows the full total.
bigGroups.sectionHint: Purchases grouped by trip, remodel, or other exceptional spending. The usual category (leisure, others, etc.) stays; here you set the group to review and filter the chart.
bigGroups.deleteConfirm: Delete group “{{label}}”? It has {{count}} assigned purchase(s); remove them from the group before deleting.
bigGroups.deleteBlockedHint: Remove every purchase from the group before deleting it.

creditCard.installmentsLedgerHint: Data imported into the database from statements (installments).
creditCard.installmentsEmpty: No installment plans or imported statements. Import PDFs with npm run import:cfraser-inbox or load the ledger with import:cc-parsed.
creditCard.activePurchasesEmpty: No installment purchases with a pending balance.
creditCard.completedPurchasesCaption: Includes contracts whose total paid reached the principal (incl. the final installment in the PDF even when <1>nro_cuota_current</1> is missing on summary rows like <2>03 CUOTAS COMERC</2>).
creditCard.completedPurchasesEmpty: No settled installment purchases in the ledger.
creditCard.statementsForPurchase: Statement(s) with an installment for this purchase:
creditCard.monthCuotasEmpty: No pending installments on the calendar.
creditCard.mortgageSheetEmpty: No payment rows in the database. Run npm run import:excel (export depto-dividendos.csv from Numbers into cfraser/ before the import).

payByRule.ledger: Installments by statement month: each row is one facturación (closing ~20th) with its pay-by date (~10th of the next month); "cuota a pagar" = plan installments billed at that close. PDFs pin amounts, progress and pay-by dates; open/projected months follow the plan. Rows roll off once their pay-by date passes.
payByRule.statementsOnly: Imported statements (PDF). No installment purchases in the ledger until CLP statements are imported.

mirrorPairs.title: Historical mirrors
mirrorPairs.intro: Mirror movement pairs (a withdrawal in one account + an equal deposit in another) that predate the transfer model. Converting a pair replaces it with a single transfer dated on the withdrawal day; the original notes are kept and the conversion is reversible. Deposits already linked in the reconciliation are left out. Note: if a converted statement credit counted as income, the income total drops (it is a correction).
mirrorPairs.blockedIntro: Not convertible: the checking/cuenta vista credit straddles the month boundary and would move the statement-calibrated balance.
mirrorPairs.badgeWideGap: outside the bank window (re-importing the statement may duplicate)
mirrorPairs.confirmBatchMessage: {{n}} pairs will be converted into single transfers. Month-end balances do not change; the operation is reversible per pair.
mirrorPairs.confirmSingleMessage: Convert {{from}} → {{to}} for {{amount}} into a single transfer.

wealthPercentile.title: Wealth percentile
wealthPercentile.intro: Your net worth's position in each country's wealth-per-adult distribution (lognormal model calibrated with the UBS Global Wealth Databooks; CLP converted at the year-end dólar observado).

markers.distributionYear: * uses the latest available databook distribution ({{year}})
markers.reconstructed: ‡ own reconstruction (not an official UBS figure)

caveats.title: Methodology notes
caveats.tail: The lognormal model underestimates the extreme tail (>p99.5); p99 thresholds are more reliable than any figure about the ultra-rich.
caveats.reconstruction: Chile 2025 is an own reconstruction (BCCh + FX + UBS Gini) validated against the UBS millionaire count, not an official UBS figure.
caveats.benchmarkReconstruction: Mexico, Brazil and Mainland China 2025 are own reconstructions from the UBS GWR 2026 Gini + millionaire counts (their means/medians fall outside the published top-30 tables).
caveats.methodologyBreak: Pre- and post-2023 medians are not comparable due to UBS's methodology change; 2023–2024 parameters are interpolated across the break.
caveats.adults: Percentiles are over each country's adult (20+) population; wealth is measured at the individual level (UBS assigns household wealth to adults).
caveats.fx: CLP→USD conversion uses the year-end BCentral dólar observado (UBS end-of-period convention), not the app-wide Yahoo rate.

projections.title: Projections
projections.intro: One trajectory in today's money: accumulation to age {{age}} (real return + monthly contribution on the invested portion; real estate and cash hold their real value) and then three comparable drawdown strategies. The nominal line uses the visible currency's inflation; both views (the global CLP/USD toggle) share the same real trajectory.

summary.potDescription: strategies on {{pot}} = invested + {{pct}}% of the rest
summary.rentIncluded: Includes passive rent of {{amount}}/mo in the income; the fixed-income strategy only draws from the pot what the rent does not cover.
summary.neverDepletes: never depletes; the income varies with the balance

projections.footnote: USD reference levels (250k–2M) converted at the current exchange rate ({{fx}} CLP/USD) in the CLP view. The whole projected chart is in today's money except the nominal line.
